articles.title: US stocks today: US market rises on Iran ceasefire extension and solid earnings
articles.contentSnippet: U.S. stocks rallied Wednesday, snapping a two-day decline, as President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran. Despite ongoing uncertainty and Iran's seizure of ships, positive earnings reports and strong tech sector performance boosted investor sentiment. The market remains hopeful for a beneficial outcome to the geopolitical situation.

articles.title: US stocks: FICO shares slide as Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae begin accepting rival VantageScore
articles.contentSnippet: FICO shares dropped 13% after Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae announced they will accept mortgages scored by rival VantageScore 4.0. This move aims to reduce homebuyer costs and increase competition in the credit scoring market. The housing giants will begin with a limited rollout to approved lenders.

articles.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
articles.contentSnippet: Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex declined, snapping a three-day rally due to IT and FMCG losses. Rising India VIX signaled caution, though broader trends remain positive with key support and resistance levels in focus.

articles.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Adani Green and HBL Engineering on Thursday for gains up to 16%
articles.contentSnippet: Nifty 50 ended lower, breaking its winning streak amid IT and FMCG weakness. Technical indicators show mixed signals with bearish bias short term. Analysts highlight support levels and recommend selective buying in Adani Green Energy and HBL Engineering.

articles.title: Only a handful of traders power India’s F&O volumes, highlights Zerodha's Nithin Kamath
articles.contentSnippet: Nithin Kamath said India’s F&O market remains small despite perceptions, with limited participation. A tiny group of 1–2% traders drives 60–70% of volumes, making brokerage revenues highly dependent on a narrow, concentrated investor base.

articles.title: India denies cash, crypto payments to Iran for Hormuz passage
articles.contentSnippet: India has denied making any payments, in cash or cryptocurrency, to Iran for safe passage of its vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. This clarification follows an incident on April 18 where two Indian vessels were fired upon by Iranian forces and forced to turn back.

articles.title: BitDelta India launches institutional-grade VDA infrastructure, targets trust-led growth in crypto market
articles.contentSnippet: BitDelta India launched as an FIU-registered VDASP, targeting India’s growing crypto market with institutional-grade infrastructure, strong security, and compliance. The platform offers unified INR trading, advanced technology, and aims to drive trust-led participation beyond metro cities.

articles.title: US Stocks: Traders place $430 million bet on lower oil price before Trump ceasefire extension
articles.contentSnippet: Traders placed a series of bets worth $430 million on a drop ​in crude prices just ​15 minutes before U.S. President Donald Trump said he would extend a ​ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday.

articles.title: US Stocks: Boeing posts smaller-than-expected quarterly loss as recovery gains traction
articles.contentSnippet: Boeing on Wednesday reported a ​much smaller first-quarter loss than analysts expected, a sign of continued operational recovery at the U.S. planemaker after years of crises that dented its reputation and left it with a mountain of debt.

articles.title: US Stocks: Blockchain billionaire Sun takes Trump family’s crypto firm to court
articles.contentSnippet: Crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun on Tuesday sued World Liberty Financial, the digital currency venture co-founded by U.S. President Donald Trump and ​his sons, alleging that World Liberty illegally froze his holdings of tokens issued by the company.

articles.title: US Stocks: Philip Morris trims annual profit forecast amid nicotine pouch uncertainty
articles.contentSnippet: Philip ​Morris International cut its annual profit forecast ​on Wednesday amid regulatory uncertainty over its Zyn nicotine pouches and rising competition in tobacco products.

articles.title: US Stocks: Best Buy taps insider Bonfig to succeed veteran Barry as CEO amid demand slowdown
articles.contentSnippet: Best Buy on Wednesday named insider Jason Bonfig as CEO, replacing longtime executive Corie Barry, in ‌a leadership change ⁠that ⁠comes as the U.S. retailer grapples with weak consumer demand and tariff-related supply-chain pressures.

articles.title: US stocks today: U.S. stocks gains after Iran ceasefire extension and robust earnings bring relief
articles.contentSnippet: Wall Street indexes climbed Wednesday as President Trump extended the Iran ceasefire, despite ongoing uncertainty over the waterway's opening. Strong earnings reports, particularly from GE Vernova and Boston Scientific, bolstered investor confidence in the U.S. consumer. Technology stocks led gains, with semiconductors reaching new highs.

articles.title: Tata Communications Q4 Results: Profit falls 75% YoY to Rs 259 crore; co declares Rs 17.5 dividend
articles.contentSnippet: Tata Communications reported a 75% YoY drop in Q4 profit to Rs 259 crore due to absence of one-off gains, despite 9% revenue growth. Higher costs and base effects pressured margins, while the company announced a Rs 17.5 dividend.

articles.title: Valuation Check: 9 midcap stocks trading well above industry PE
articles.contentSnippet: Midcap stocks with P/E ratios above industry averages signal strong growth expectations and investor confidence. However, elevated valuations may also indicate overpricing risks. This list highlights ten such NSE stocks, excluding financials, identified through valuation screening.
